outlook.headline: Accelerator demand stays hot, but momentum is leveling off
outlook.summary: GPU/accelerator demand remains broadly strong across the stack, led by NVIDIA’s fastest-ever Blackwell ramp, TSMC’s expected AI accelerator revenue growth, and rising HBM/DRAM content at Micron. The near-term benefit still accrues to the core platform and ecosystem suppliers, but the 2.5x mention rate is 5% below trend, signaling demand is still elevated rather than accelerating further.

drivers.label: Blackwell / next-gen GPU ramp
drivers.detail: Frontier labs and hyperscalers are still pulling hard on the newest accelerator cycle, keeping platform demand hot.

drivers.label: Rising memory content per accelerator
drivers.detail: Next-gen GPU/XPU platforms are consuming materially more DRAM/HBM, supporting demand for memory content per system.

drivers.label: Tight downstream capacity in test, packaging, and power
drivers.detail: Order flow and qualification activity point to continued capacity pressure and strong pull-through for enablement suppliers.

drivers.label: Inference and rack-scale deployments broaden the market
drivers.detail: Demand is no longer just training-led; inference and larger system deployments are expanding the accelerator addressable market.

risks: Hyperscaler and large-customer shipment timing could slip, especially where visibility is still limited later in the year.
If supply constraints ease faster than expected, the tightness that is supporting suppliers could fade.
The slight decline in mention momentum versus trend suggests sentiment is strong but not re-accelerating; watch for further cooling.
